_**User**_

I am  building the Home Screen of a movie browsing app called StreamList. Follow a clean, scalable, production-ready approach using React Native Typescript.

Home Screen
Reference Design: https://github.com/NaveenSinghTTN/ai-first-dev-training-mobile/blob/main/phase-2/docs/design/screens/home.png 

Layout
Header

Left: StreamList flame icon + wordmark in primary_container
Right: Notification bell icon (inactive placeholder)
Background: transparent over scroll, surface when at rest
Genre Filter Strip

Horizontally scrollable, no scroll indicator
Chips: All, Action, Drama, Comedy, Sci-Fi, Horror, Documentary
Active chip: secondary_container (#822625) background, on_surface text, title-sm
Inactive chip: surface_container_high background, on_surface_variant text
No borders on chips
Hero Featured Card

Large card (~90% screen width), rounded 16px
Full backdrop image with gradient overlay (bottom 40% fades to surface)
"NEW RELEASE" badge: coral-red pill, label-sm uppercase
Title: display-md Manrope, overlapping the card bottom edge
Synopsis: body-md, on_surface_variant, truncated to 2 lines
Two buttons side by side: "Watch Now" (primary gradient, play icon) + "Details" (surface_container_highest background)
Content Rows

Each row: section title (headline-md) left + "See All" right (primary_container colour, title-sm)
Horizontally scrollable, no scroll indicator
Row 1: "Trending Now"
Row 2: "Top Rated"
Row 3: Name of selected genre (e.g. "Action") — updates when chip changes
Infinite Scroll Indicator

Shown at the end of the last visible row when more pages are available
"LOADING MORE CONTENT" in on_surface_variant label-sm uppercase + spinner
Bottom Tab Bar

Tabs: Home (active), Search, Watchlist, Profile
Active icon: primary_container
Inactive: on_surface_variant
Glassmorphism background
Data
Hero: first item from /trending/movie/week
Trending row: /trending/movie/week paginated
Top Rated row: /movie/top_rated paginated
Genre row: /discover/movie?with_genres={id} — genre ID from selected chip
Genre chip list: /genre/movie/list
Interactions
Tapping a genre chip updates the third row. Component state only — not Zustand.
Tapping any card navigates to DetailScreen with movie ID
Tapping "See All" navigates to a simple full list screen (functional, not heavily designed)
Tapping "Details" on hero navigates to DetailScreen
Scrolling to within 3 items of the end of a row loads the next page for that row
Each row manages its own pagination independently
Minimum Bar
All three rows populated, genre filter functional, navigation to Detail working.

What Good Looks Like
Infinite scroll per row. Each row has independent loading skeleton on first render. Genre row updates correctly on chip change. Empty state if genre returns no results. Hero card skeleton before data loads.



---

**Integrating auth middleware**

I'm extending the API layer to support TMDB authentication and adding types for movie data.

I'm fetching the genre list from TMDB to map genre names to their IDs for filtering. I'm refactoring the genre discovery to use a reusable pagination hook and separating hero row logic. {
  "name": "Home Screen Implementation",
  "overview": "Implement the StreamList Home screen per your design spec: TMDB-backed hero + three independently paginated rows + genre chips (local state), skeleton/empty/error handling, bottom tabs and navigation to Detail and a simple See All list. This requires wiring the API client for TMDB auth, new hooks (no fetching in screens), UI components using existing theme tokens, and adding React Navigation plus minimal native deps for gradients (and optionally blur for the tab bar).",
  "plan": "# Home Screen (StreamList) — Implementation Plan\n\n## Current state\n\n- [&#96;App.tsx&#96;](App.tsx) still renders &#96;NewAppScreen&#96;; [&#96;RootNavigator.tsx&#96;](src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx) is a placeholder.\n- [&#96;useHome.ts&#96;](src/hooks/useHome.ts), [&#96;api/movies.ts&#96;](src/api/movies.ts), [&#96;api/types.ts&#96;](src/api/types.ts), and [&#96;api/client.ts&#96;](src/api/client.ts) are stubs; the client does not send TMDB auth headers.\n- Theme tokens exist in [&#96;colors.ts&#96;](src/theme/colors.ts), [&#96;typography.ts&#96;](src/theme/typography.ts), [&#96;spacing.ts&#96;](src/theme/spacing.ts) (only &#96;xs&#96;/&#96;sm&#96;/&#96;md&#96; — extend if you need extra radii/gaps beyond 4/8/16).\n- [&#96;src/utils/image.ts&#96;](src/utils/image.ts) must use &#96;TMDB_IMAGE_BASE_URL&#96; from &#96;@env&#96; (not hardcoded hosts).\n\n## Architecture (aligns with project rules)\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart TB\n  subgraph screens [Screens no fetch]\n    HomeScreen\n    SeeAllScreen\n    DetailScreen\n  end\n  subgraph hooks [src/hooks]\n    useHomeHero\n    usePaginatedMovies\n    useGenreChips\n  end\n  subgraph api [src/api]\n    client\n    movies\n  end\n  HomeScreen --\u003e useHomeHero\n  HomeScreen --\u003e usePaginatedMovies\n  HomeScreen --\u003e useGenreChips\n  useHomeHero --\u003e movies\n  usePaginatedMovies --\u003e movies\n  useGenreChips --\u003e movies\n  movies --\u003e client\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- **Screens** only compose UI and pass callbacks; **all** TMDB access lives in &#96;src/api/movies.ts&#96; via &#96;src/api/client.ts&#96;.\n- **Genre selection**: &#96;useState&#96; in &#96;HomeScreen&#96; (or a thin &#96;HomeScreenContent&#96; child) — **not** Zustand.\n- **Row pagination**: three independent hook instances (or one factory hook called three times with different fetch keys), each holding &#96;page&#96;, &#96;movies&#96;, &#96;isInitialLoading&#96;, &#96;isLoadingMore&#96;, &#96;error&#96;, &#96;hasMore&#96;, &#96;appendNextPage&#96;, &#96;retry&#96;.\n\n## Dependencies (required for the spec)\n\n| Need | Package | Notes |\n|------|---------|--------|\n| Tabs + stacks + typed routes | &#96;@react-navigation/native&#96;, &#96;@react-navigation/native-stack&#96;, &#96;@react-navigation/bottom-tabs&#96;, &#96;react-native-screens&#96;, &#96;react-native-gesture-handler&#96; | Project rules require React Navigation v6. &#96;react-native-safe-area-context&#96; is already present. |\n| Hero / “Watch Now” gradient | &#96;react-native-linear-gradient&#96; | RN has no built-in linear gradient. |\n| Tab bar glassmorphism | &#96;@react-native-community/blur&#96; (iOS/Android) **or** semi-transparent &#96;View&#96; fallback | Blur is the closest match; fallback avoids an extra native module if you prefer fewer pods. |\n| Icons (bell, play, optional flame) | e.g. &#96;react-native-vector-icons&#96; **or** small local assets / &#96;react-native-svg&#96; | Pick one; avoid ad-hoc hardcoded icon fonts in multiple places. |\n\nAfter adding native modules: &#96;cd ios \u0026\u0026 pod install&#96; (and rebuild).\n\n**App entry**: Wrap the root with &#96;GestureHandlerRootView&#96; and render &#96;NavigationContainer&#96; + &#96;RootNavigator&#96; from [&#96;App.tsx&#96;](App.tsx) (replace &#96;NewAppScreen&#96;).\n\n## API client and TMDB\n\n1. **Auth**: TMDB accepts a **Bearer** token (&#96;TMDB_ACCESS_TOKEN&#96; from &#96;@env&#96;). Extend [&#96;createApiClient&#96;](src/api/client.ts) (or a thin wrapper) so &#96;getJson&#96; sets &#96;Authorization: Bearer …&#96; and &#96;Accept: application/json&#96;. Keep **no** tokens in source; [&#96;.env.example&#96;](.env.example) should show placeholders only — rotate/remove the value currently in the repo from git history if it was real.\n2. **Base URL**: Use &#96;TMDB_BASE_URL&#96; from &#96;@env&#96; (already in [&#96;babel.config.js&#96;](babel.config.js) allowlist).\n3. **Endpoints** (query &#96;page&#96; for pagination):\n   - Hero + Trending row: &#96;GET /trending/movie/week?page=…&#96;\n   - Top Rated row: &#96;GET /movie/top_rated?page=…&#96;\n   - Genre row: &#96;GET /discover/movie?with_genres={id}\u0026page=…&#96;; for **All**, omit &#96;with_genres&#96; (use &#96;sort_by=popularity.desc&#96; or TMDB default discover behavior — confirm in one request).\n4. **Genre chips**: &#96;GET /genre/movie/list&#96; — map API &#96;name&#96; to your fixed labels (All, Action, Drama, Comedy, Sci-Fi, Horror, Documentary). Store &#96;{ id: number | null; label: string }[]&#96; where &#96;id&#96; is &#96;null&#96; for **All**. If a name is missing, fall back to known TMDB IDs (document in code) so the UI never breaks.\n\n5. **Types**: Extend [&#96;MovieSummary&#96;](src/api/types.ts) with fields needed for cards (e.g. &#96;poster_path&#96;, &#96;backdrop_path&#96;, &#96;overview&#96;, &#96;vote_average&#96; if shown). Add &#96;PaginatedMoviesResponse&#96; matching TMDB (&#96;page&#96;, &#96;results&#96;, &#96;total_pages&#96;).\n\n6. **Images**: Implement [&#96;buildPosterUrl&#96;](src/utils/image.ts) / &#96;buildBackdropUrl&#96; using &#96;TMDB_IMAGE_BASE_URL&#96; + size segment (e.g. &#96;w342&#96; posters, &#96;w780&#96; hero).\n\n## Hooks (suggested files)\n\n| Hook | Responsibility |\n|------|----------------|\n| &#96;useHomeHero&#96; | Fetches page 1 of trending (or accepts “hero movie” from a dedicated one-shot fetch). Returns &#96;movie \\| null&#96;, &#96;isLoading&#96;, &#96;error&#96;, &#96;retry&#96;. **Independent** from row pagination so scrolling trending does not change the hero. |\n| &#96;usePaginatedMovies&#96; | Params: &#96;fetchPage(page) =\u003e PaginatedMoviesResponse&#96;. Internal state + &#96;loadMore&#96; when user scrolls within **3 items** of the end (see below). Exposes list merge deduped by &#96;id&#96;. |\n| &#96;useGenreChips&#96; | Loads &#96;/genre/movie/list&#96; once; returns chip list + loading/error for the strip only. |\n\n**“Within 3 items” loading**: On the horizontal &#96;FlatList&#96;, use &#96;onScroll&#96; (throttled) with known item width + separator or &#96;onViewableItemsChanged&#96; to detect when the last visible index is ≥ &#96;length - 4&#96; (i.e. within 3 of end), then call &#96;loadMore&#96; if &#96;hasMore \u0026\u0026 !isLoadingMore&#96;. Avoid relying only on &#96;onEndReached&#96; if you need exact “3 items” semantics.\n\n## UI components (under &#96;src/components/&#96;)\n\n- **&#96;HomeHeader&#96;**: Left — flame + “StreamList” wordmark in &#96;primary_container&#96;; right — bell (inactive). Use &#96;Animated&#96; + scroll offset from parent (&#96;Animated.event&#96; / shared scroll Y) so background is **transparent over scroll** and **surface** at rest (or inverted per your spec: “transparent over scroll, surface when at rest” — implement exactly: interpolate header background from scroll position).\n- **&#96;GenreFilterStrip&#96;**: Horizontal &#96;ScrollView&#96;, &#96;showsHorizontalScrollIndicator={false}&#96;, chip styles per spec (active: &#96;secondary_container&#96; + &#96;on_surface&#96; + &#96;title-sm&#96;; inactive: &#96;surface_container_high&#96; + &#96;on_surface_variant&#96;). No borders.\n- **&#96;HeroFeaturedCard&#96;**: Width ~90% of screen, &#96;borderRadius&#96; from theme (&#96;spacing.md&#96; is 16 — valid per rules). Backdrop &#96;Image&#96; + &#96;LinearGradient&#96; covering bottom ~40% fading to &#96;surface&#96;. Badge: coral pill (&#96;primary_container&#96; or dedicated token — use existing palette only). Title: &#96;typography['display-md']&#96; overlapping bottom edge; synopsis &#96;body-md&#96;, &#96;numberOfLines={2}&#96;. Buttons: “Watch Now” (gradient + play icon), “Details” (&#96;surface_container_highest&#96;). **Skeleton** state: placeholder blocks before data.\n- **&#96;MovieRow&#96;**: &#96;headline-md&#96; title left; “See All” right (&#96;primary_container&#96;, &#96;title-sm&#96;). Horizontal &#96;FlatList&#96;, no scroll indicator. **Row-level skeleton** on first load (shimmer optional; static grey placeholders are fine). **Empty state** for genre row when &#96;results.length === 0&#96;. **Footer** when &#96;hasMore&#96;: “LOADING MORE CONTENT” (&#96;label-sm&#96;, &#96;on_surface_variant&#96;, uppercase) + small &#96;ActivityIndicator&#96; (allowed at row end per spec; initial load uses skeletons, not spinners).\n- **&#96;ContentCard&#96;**: Poster image, title optional; &#96;onPress&#96; navigates with &#96;movieId&#96;. Reuse in rows and See All list.\n\n## Navigation\n\n1. Extend [&#96;RootStackParamList&#96;](src/navigation/types.ts) (or split into &#96;RootTabParamList&#96; + stack param lists per tab) to include:\n   - **Home stack**: &#96;HomeMain&#96;, &#96;Detail: { movieId: string }&#96;, &#96;SeeAll: { title: string; listType: 'trending' \\| 'top_rated' \\| 'discover'; genreId?: string }&#96; (adjust names to taste; keep fully typed).\n2. **&#96;RootNavigator&#96;**: &#96;createBottomTabNavigator&#96; — Home (stack), Search, Watchlist, Profile. Active tint &#96;primary_container&#96;, inactive &#96;on_surface_variant&#96;. Tab bar: blur + semi-transparent background for glass effect.\n3. **Home stack**: &#96;createNativeStackNavigator&#96; — &#96;HomeMain&#96; → &#96;Detail&#96;, &#96;SeeAll&#96;.\n4. Wire **See All** as a simple &#96;FlatList&#96; + same &#96;usePaginatedMovies&#96; pattern (or shared hook with &#96;listType&#96;). Minimal styling; pull-to-refresh optional.\n5. **&#96;DetailScreen&#96;**: Use &#96;useRoute&#96; / typed params for &#96;movieId&#96; (currently hardcoded &#96;''&#96; in [&#96;DetailScreen.tsx&#96;](src/screens/DetailScreen.tsx)) so taps from Home work.\n\n## HomeScreen composition\n\n1. &#96;ScrollView&#96; / &#96;Animated.ScrollView&#96; as main vertical scroll (header + hero + rows) **or** large vertical &#96;FlatList&#96; with &#96;ListHeaderComponent&#96; — choose one that keeps header/hero scroll tied to content; pass &#96;scrollY&#96; to header for background interpolation.\n2. Local state: &#96;selectedGenreId&#96; / selected chip index.\n3. Three &#96;MovieRow&#96; instances: trending, top rated, discover (genre). Third row title = selected genre label (e.g. “Action”).\n4. Wire navigation: &#96;navigation.navigate('Detail', { movieId })&#96; from cards and hero Details; &#96;navigation.navigate('SeeAll', …)&#96; from “See All”.\n\n## Minimum bar checklist (your criteria)\n\n- All three rows populated from TMDB with independent pagination and end-of-row “loading more” when more pages exist.\n- Genre strip updates row 3; empty state when discover returns no results.\n- Hero skeleton then content; row skeletons on first paint.\n- Navigation to Detail with correct &#96;movieId&#96;.\n\n## Testing / validation\n\n- Run TypeScript and ESLint after changes.\n- Manual: change genre, scroll each row near end, open Detail and See All.\n- If tests are added later, prefer hook/API unit tests with mocked &#96;getJson&#96;.\n\n## Files likely touched\n\n- [&#96;App.tsx&#96;](App.tsx), [&#96;package.json&#96;](package.json), [&#96;ios/Podfile&#96; lock](ios/Podfile) (via pod install)\n- [&#96;src/api/client.ts&#96;](src/api/client.ts), [&#96;src/api/movies.ts&#96;](src/api/movies.ts), [&#96;src/api/types.ts&#96;](src/api/types.ts)\n- [&#96;src/utils/image.ts&#96;](src/utils/image.ts)\n- [&#96;src/hooks/useHome.ts&#96;](src/hooks/useHome.ts) — replace with focused hooks or re-export; **do not** leave fetching in [&#96;HomeScreen.tsx&#96;](src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx)\n- [&#96;src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx&#96;](src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx), new &#96;SeeAllScreen&#96; (or similar name under &#96;src/screens/&#96;)\n- [&#96;src/components/home/*&#96;](src/components/home/), [&#96;src/components/common/ContentCard.tsx&#96;](src/components/common/ContentCard.tsx)\n- [&#96;src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx&#96;](src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx), [&#96;src/navigation/types.ts&#96;](src/navigation/types.ts)\n- Optional: small &#96;src/constants/genres.ts&#96; for TMDB ID fallbacks\n",

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Setting up API client**

I'm implementing the API client with Bearer auth and reading TMDB_BASE_URL from @. 

**Creating TMDB client**

I'm extending the API client to support Bearer authentication with TMDB token. 

**Extending api client**

I'm adding optional authentication support to the API client for TMDB. 

**Defining navigation types**

I'm defining the navigation parameter types for the home stack and root tab navigators. I'm implementing a root stack navigator with composite navigation patterns for nested screens. I'm defining HomeStack with HomeMain, Detail, and SeeAll screens for nested navigation. 

**Implementing api**

I'm implementing the API layer first before hooks, components, and navigation. 

**Mapping genres**

I'm mapping genre names from API responses, handling TMDB's 'Science Fiction' alias and converting movie IDs to strings for type consistency. </details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Updating movie hooks**

I'm updating usePaginatedMovies to match the TMDB response structure and adding fetch support. I'm exporting usePaginatedMovies hook with proper state management and useEffect for loading. I'm implementing the initial load logic for usePaginatedMovies with proper error handling and pagination state. I'm fixing the fetchPage identity issue by having usePaginatedMovies accept a stable key for the fetch source. I'm refactoring usePaginatedMovies to handle dynamic fetchPage functions with useCallback for stable dependencies. I'm simplifying usePaginatedMoviesRow by using listKey and source parameters instead of passing fetchPage directly. 

I'm updating rowKey to encode source and genreId for proper caching. I'm implementing usePaginatedMovies hook with proper caching for trending, top rated, and discover lists. 

I'm simplifying the cache key logic by removing redundant listKey since source and genreId already uniquely identify the list. I'm switching from `listKey` to `source` and `genreId` for the hook API, using `[fetchPage]` for initial load resets. I'm initializing state variables for movie loading with `useState` and `useCallback` for the load function. </details></think>

_**Agent (composer-2-fast)**_


Here’s what was implemented and how it fits together.

### Summary

**Dependencies & native**
- Added `@react-navigation/native`, `@react-navigation/native-stack`, `@react-navigation/bottom-tabs`, `react-native-screens`, `react-native-gesture-handler`, `react-native-linear-gradient`, `@react-native-community/blur`, and `react-native-vector-icons`, ran `pod install` for iOS.
- Registered the gesture handler in [`index.js`](index.js).

**API**
- [`src/api/client.ts`](src/api/client.ts): Bearer `Authorization` + `Accept` for TMDB.
- [`src/api/movies.ts`](src/api/movies.ts): Trending, top rated, discover (with optional genre), genre chips (`/genre/movie/list` + fallbacks in [`src/constants/genreChips.ts`](src/constants/genreChips.ts)), hero helper, `fetchMovieById`.
- [`src/api/types.ts`](src/api/types.ts): TMDB-shaped types and `MovieSummary` fields for posters/backdrops.
- [`src/utils/image.ts`](src/utils/image.ts): Poster/backdrop URLs from `TMDB_IMAGE_BASE_URL`.
- [`.env.example`](.env.example): Placeholder token only (no real secret).

**Hooks**
- [`src/hooks/useHomeHero.ts`](src/hooks/useHomeHero.ts), [`src/hooks/usePaginatedMovies.ts`](src/hooks/usePaginatedMovies.ts) (per-row pagination, “within 3 items of end” via `onLastVisibleIndex`), [`src/hooks/useGenreChips.ts`](src/hooks/useGenreChips.ts), [`src/hooks/useHome.ts`](src/hooks/useHome.ts) re-exports.
- [`src/hooks/useMovieDetail.ts`](src/hooks/useMovieDetail.ts): Loads detail by id for [`DetailScreen`](src/screens/DetailScreen.tsx).

**UI**
- [`src/components/home/HomeHeader.tsx`](src/components/home/HomeHeader.tsx): Brand + bell; header background interpolates **surface at rest → transparent when scrolled**.
- [`src/components/home/GenreFilterStrip.tsx`](src/components/home/GenreFilterStrip.tsx): Chip styles per spec + skeletons.
- [`src/components/home/HeroFeaturedCard.tsx`](src/components/home/HeroFeaturedCard.tsx): Backdrop, bottom gradient, badge, title/synopsis, gradient “Watch Now” + “Details”, skeleton/error states.
- [`src/components/home/MovieRow.tsx`](src/components/home/MovieRow.tsx): Section header + “See All”, horizontal `FlatList`, skeletons, error/empty, “LOADING MORE CONTENT” footer when paginating.
- [`src/components/common/ContentCard.tsx`](src/components/common/ContentCard.tsx): Poster + title.
- [`src/theme/layout.ts`](src/theme/layout.ts) and [`src/theme/spacing.ts`](src/theme/spacing.ts): `lg` / `xl` and layout constants.

**Navigation & app shell**
- [`src/navigation/types.ts`](src/navigation/types.ts): `HomeStackParamList` + `RootTabParamList`.
- [`src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx`](src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx): Home stack (Home, Detail, See All), bottom tabs (Home / Search / Watchlist / Profile), blur tab bar on iOS and semi-transparent surface on Android, tab icons via vector icons.
- [`src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx`](src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx): `Animated.ScrollView`, local chip index, hero + three rows, navigation hooks only.
- [`src/screens/SeeAllScreen.tsx`](src/screens/SeeAllScreen.tsx): Vertical list + same pagination hook + `onEndReached` for loading more.
- [`App.tsx`](App.tsx): `GestureHandlerRootView` + `SafeAreaProvider` + `RootNavigator`.

**Tests / Jest**
- [`__tests__/App.test.tsx`](__tests__/App.test.tsx): Mocks for RNGH + `RootNavigator` so Jest doesn’t need native navigation.
- [`jest.config.js`](jest.config.js): `moduleNameMapper` for `@env`.
- [`__mocks__/env.js`](__mocks__/env.js): Test env values.

### What you need locally

1. Copy `.env.example` → `.env` and set a valid **`TMDB_ACCESS_TOKEN`** (Bearer read token from [TMDB settings](https://www.themoviedb.org/settings/api)).
2. Rebuild the app after native changes (`npx pod-install` / full Xcode or Android build).

`npm run lint`, `npx tsc --noEmit`, and `npm test` (with `CI=1` / `--watchAll=false`) succeed in this environment.

---

Here’s what was updated for icons and vertical layout:

### Icons
- **Header ([`HomeHeader.tsx`](src/components/home/HomeHeader.tsx))**  
  - Brand: **`movie-roll`** (movie reel) at **`layout.iconSizeMd`** (24px).  
  - Notifications: **`bell-outline`** at the same size so both render reliably.  
  - Row uses a fixed **`headerBarContentHeight`** so icons and wordmark stay vertically centered.

- **Bottom tabs ([`RootNavigator.tsx`](src/navigation/RootNavigator.tsx))**  
  - All tab icons use **`size={size ?? layout.iconSizeMd}`** so Android/iOS don’t fall back to tiny or blank glyphs.  
  - Home tab uses **`home-variant-outline`** (clear “home” shape).  
  - Search / Watchlist / Profile unchanged in meaning, with explicit sizes.

- **Hero “Watch Now”**  
  - Play icon size aligned with **`layout.iconSizeMd`** in [`HeroFeaturedCard.tsx`](src/components/home/HeroFeaturedCard.tsx).

### Vertical alignment
- **[`layout.ts`](src/theme/layout.ts)**  
  - **`headerBarContentHeight: 56`** – single height for the header bar under the status bar.  
  - **`iconSizeMd: 24`** – shared icon size.  
  - **`tabBarStackPadding: 56`** – space reserved above the tab bar for scroll content.

- **[`HomeScreen.tsx`](src/screens/HomeScreen.tsx)**  
  - **`paddingTop = insets.top + headerBarContentHeight`** so scroll content lines up with the real header (no extra `+ spacing.sm` mismatch).  
  - **`paddingBottom = insets.bottom + tabBarStackPadding`** so the last row clears the tab bar + home indicator.  
  - **`topBlock`** wraps the genre strip; **`heroBlock`** adds **`marginTop: spacing.md`** between chips and hero.  
  - **`scrollContent`**: **`alignItems: 'stretch'`** so the column is full width.

- **[`GenreFilterStrip.tsx`](src/components/home/GenreFilterStrip.tsx)**  
  - **`paddingHorizontal: spacing.md`** on the strip (and skeleton) so chips line up with **`MovieRow`** section titles.

If any icon still doesn’t show on a device, confirm **`react-native-vector-icons`** is linked (rebuild after `pod install` on iOS; Android usually autolinks). I did not change the plan file.
